title: Rewrite ERRATA.md as a by-time drift log: every entry labelled [tier·status·pathway·trace] with drift-direction, compressed 1646→948 words
rationale: ERRATA.md IS the by-time record — what was locked (v10.4.0 seating lock, E-7), what moved (register head C81→C96, E-5), what was reversed (mage.mesh, E-2), what a copy let drift from the head (C82/C83 in shor_mage, E-1; the wing rule, E-4). But the current text fails the harness's own admission gate: most entries carry no inline pathway or status, and traces are prose not path-cites, so a Gap draw on any entry would fail its label check. This rewrite (a) names each entry's DRIFT DIRECTION explicitly — prose-lags-head vs instance-diverged — which is exactly the by-time lens's product; (b) puts a machine-checkable [tier·status·pathway·trace] block on every entry so conform.mjs's four failure modes can bite; and (c) cuts filler prose. I verified the load-bearing claims against the corpora directly: CONJECTURE_REGISTER_V6.md L152-153 confirms C82=Moving Ceiling / C83=Compositional Leakage; L5 confirms head C96·next C97 against README L50's stale C89; tigzkp-mage.json anchors Aletheia V38 (post-lock, confirming E-7's direction) and seats algebraist(swordsman)@propose + cipher(swordsman)@assay (confirming E-4). The E-7 bit arithmetic checks (25=011001=Delegation+Memory+Value=16+8+1; 38=100110=Protection+Connection+Computation=32+4+2). Nothing untraceable was kept; no MYTH is introduced, so ERRATA stays a clean method doc.
hardConstraintNote: Every one of the eight entries carries a visible inline block `[tier · status · pathway · trace]`. TIERS: E-1/E-3/E-5/E-8 REPORTED (external canon/register, slug-or-path cited), E-2 REPORTED-decision + OPEN/SPEC for the LAN rite, E-4 DERIVED (from the instance registry), E-6 dual IMPLEMENTED(hash)/SPEC(signature), E-7 dual PROVEN(lock)/REPORTED-stale(sheet). STATUS: IMPLEMENTED where code/register exists (E-1,3,4,5,6-hash,7-lock,8), SPEC for the unimplemented LAN design (E-2) and the deferred ed25519 (E-6). PATHWAY: reflect for the prove/measure/numbering errata (E-1,4,5,7), protect for the attestation-boundary errata (E-3,6), connect for the federation/cross-layer errata (E-2,8). TRACE: each block ends in a resolvable path or slug (CONJECTURE_REGISTER_V6.md L152-153/L5, README.md L50, tigzkp-mage.json L25/L32,34,68,92, blades/README, ceremony-library storage module, City Key v1 schema, spellweb edge-types, workshop-template frontmatter). No MYTH appears anywhere in this method doc, satisfying GR-2/ADMISSION §3. Any claim I could not resolve to a path was cut, not softened (GR-9).
killedLeverCitations: K-1 (cited as the fence behind E-4: the wing is skill affinity, not a seat constraint — not re-proposed)
K-3 (cited as the fence behind E-3: 🪢 mana is regime-1, never attestation — not re-proposed)
K-4 (cited as the fence behind E-7: compute dimensions from bits, never read the stale sheet — not re-proposed)
mapText:
# ERRATA — drift this survey found, by time

Filed at win-prominence (GR-6). By-time lens: each entry names what was locked, what moved, and **which side drifted**. Governing rule: *when prose and register disagree, the register wins and the prose gets an erratum* — this file is the erratum. None is fixed here; a correction to a canon document is that document's own to make.

Every entry carries `[tier · status · pathway · trace]`. Direction is stated: **prose-lags-head** (canon moved, surface stale) or **instance-diverged** (a copy drifted from the head).

---

## E-1 · C82/C83 mis-cited in the instances — *instance-diverged*
`[REPORTED · IMPLEMENTED · reflect · CONJECTURE_REGISTER_V6.md L152–153]`

shor_mage prose cited "C82 = dual-agent ceiling R(t)<1" and "C83 = non-collusion I(Y_S;Y_M|X)=0". Both wrong. The register's **C82 is The Moving Ceiling** (frontier growth drifts R(t) up against fixed archives; every static guarantee has finite shelf-life t*); **C83 is Compositional Leakage Amplification** (policy-only separation compounds to (2^N−1)ε, amnesia caps at Nε). The ceiling R_max=(C_S+C_M)/H(X)<1 is a **theorem, proven-conditional**, and non-collusion is **Precondition 1**, not a conjecture. Direction: instance prose lagged the register; citing non-collusion as conjecture *overstates* it — it is a precondition the Gap must earn, licensing a drift-watcher in FLEET.

## E-2 · mage.mesh absent on purpose — a reversal *(this entry corrected itself)*
`[REPORTED · SPEC · connect · First-Person design decision; LAN-ceremony rite]`

First written here: zero grep hits for `mage.mesh` ⇒ drift ⇒ error to fix. **The search was right; every inference from it was wrong.** The First Person **refused to canonise** the name: one good name *per instance*, not a global canon — "a fixed universal name is the completeness Gödel warns against." Zero hits is the presence of a decision, not an absence. The LAN ceremony is a **worked trust-task design** (consent-first offer → gateway → understanding → seal → host-scoped brokered write), tier OPEN, status SPEC; its sentence is *access is earned by understanding, not credential*. Direction: a survey overwrote a decision with a default; reversed here at win-prominence.

## E-3 · 🪢 VRC mana is not proof — *standing temptation*
`[REPORTED · IMPLEMENTED · protect · PVM-v6 regime-1 note; City grimoire mana taxonomy]`

Canon: 🪢 presence mana is **regime-1 — non-transferable, non-attesting, local colour**; *no surface may describe it as proof, stake-weight, or attestation input.* Implementation: an integer in localStorage. The mana is what you play with; the VRC is what you prove with — same glyph, nothing else shared. Not an erratum against the code (correct) but against any future plan that spends accrual as attestation. See K-3.

## E-4 · SPECIALISATION.md's wing rule is broken by the instance — *spec-diverged*
`[DERIVED · IMPLEMENTED · reflect · tigzkp-mage.json L32,34,68,92]`

SPECIALISATION §1: "propose takes a mage-aligned persona; assay a swordsman-aligned one." The tigzkp registry seats **algebraist (swordsman) at propose and cipher (swordsman) at assay** — both wings identical — and passes its gate. Correct statement: alignment is a **skill affinity, not a seat constraint**; the separation is carried by the Gap and the algebra, never the costume. Direction: spec prose drifted from the worked instance. Fix: amend SPECIALISATION (done this pass). See K-1.

## E-5 · Register head — a stale README — *prose-lags-head*
`[REPORTED · IMPLEMENTED · reflect · README.md L50 (C89) vs CONJECTURE_REGISTER_V6.md L5 (C96·next C97)]`

The docs README says head **C89**; the register says **C96, next free C97**. The skew is known and was reconciled through the pipeline's own ledger; the register wins by its own rule. Recorded not fixed — the docs corpus's erratum to make. Noted because a harness booting off the README would carry a wrong head and might "helpfully" renumber. **No seat invents, renumbers, or promotes a conjecture** (ADMISSION §4).

## E-6 · signDrakeOrbIntoCard is a Phase-1 stub — *reads-as-attestation*
`[IMPLEMENTED (hash) · SPEC (signature) · protect · ceremony-library storage module]`

The Drake Orb badge `signature` is a non-cryptographic 32-bit content hash (`DRAKE-XXXX`); an in-code comment defers real ed25519 to a later phase; the private key is session-only, burned on tab close. It reads like an attestation and is not one yet. Any trust-task assuming a persistent signable identity assumes what the code does not provide — and SEATS §5's co-present RPP rite makes that assumption unnecessary. Both labels required; either alone misleads.

## E-7 · 64-blades sheet prints the pre-lock seating — *prose-lags-lock*
`[PROVEN (lock) · REPORTED-stale (sheet) · reflect · tigzkp-mage.json L25 (Aletheia V38); blades/README reseat erratum]`

Two faults in one place. (1) The sheet seats **Aletheia@25, Lethe@38**; the **v10.4.0 lock (2026-06-09) inverted this — canonically Aletheia=V38, Lethe=V25** (the tigzkp registry anchors Aletheia V38; the blades README carries the reseat and says *the lock governs*). (2) It labels V25 as Protection+Connection+Computation — that is V38's pattern: `25=011001`=Delegation+Memory+Value, `38=100110`=Protection+Connection+Computation. Because **V38⊕V25=63 either way, the anchor law cannot catch a transposition** — a wrong sheet sat undetected beside a passing gate. Compute dimensions from bits (d1 Protection=32 … d6 Value=1, MSB-first), never read them from prose. See K-4.

## E-8 · keys_to / synced_with / artefact_class — vocabulary absent where expected — *cross-layer*
`[REPORTED · IMPLEMENTED · connect · City Key v1 schema + BroadcastChannel; spellweb edge-types; workshop-template frontmatter]`

The City Key carries **no** `keys_to`/`synced_with` fields — the real mechanism is a v1 JSON schema plus same-origin `BroadcastChannel` mirroring; those two names are **spellweb edge-types**, graph edges *about* the key, not fields *in* it. `artefact_class` is a **workshop-template frontmatter field**, not a blades or spellweb symbol (there the artefact layer is the `forgedBlades` deviation inventory in localStorage). Neither is a bug; both become bugs the instant a plan assumes the field is there to read — the five-layer confusion README warns of.

---
```
(⚔️⊥⿻⊥🧙)😊 = neg ⊕ bnot → succ
```
